title: High-energy kitten
summary: A short hunting game for a young cat.
steps: Prepare a wand toy without small loose parts.
Move it low on the floor for 2-3 minutes.
End with a calm catch.
warnings: Do not use hands as toys.

title: Plan for a very active kitten
summary: A safe session: hunt, catch, calm down and reward.
steps: Clear cables and small objects from the floor.
Start with slow toy movement.
Change pace for 5 minutes: escape, stop, hide.
Let the kitten catch the toy several times.
Finish with a small treat or meal portion.
Note which game worked best.
dontDo: do not use a laser without a catchable reward

title: Calm play for a senior cat
summary: Gentle activity without jumps or joint strain.
steps: Keep the toy on floor level.
Move it slowly over a short distance.
Stop if the cat loses interest or moves with difficulty.
warnings: Pain, limping or sudden apathy needs veterinary advice.

title: Senior cat — gentle daily plan
summary: A plan combining movement, sniffing and calm interaction.
steps: In the morning, do 3 minutes of floor-level toy movement.
At midday, hide 2-3 treats in easy places.
In the evening, repeat a short no-jump game.
Watch breathing, movement and willingness to participate.
End with petting only if the cat asks for it.
dontDo: do not extend the session by force

title: Hunt game for an active cat
summary: A fast session to release energy.
steps: Choose a wand toy or ball.
Make several short chases.
Let the cat win and catch the toy.
warnings: Do not push the cat into panting or frustration.

title: Active cat — full hunting session
summary: Play cycle: stalk, chase, catch and calm down.
steps: Move the toy very slowly for one minute.
Run short floor-level chases for 3-5 minutes.
Hide the toy behind a chair or box.
Let the cat catch it every few attempts.
Finish with a small food portion.
Give the cat 10 minutes of quiet time.
dontDo: do not take the toy away immediately after a catch

title: Cardboard hideout
steps: Use a clean box without staples.
Cut one entrance and a small window.
Add a light ball or paper ball.
warnings: Do not use foil, rubber bands or sharp parts.

title: Cardboard play zone
summary: A home toy for hiding, stalking and resting.
steps: Check that the box is clean and stable.
Cut an entrance and two small viewing holes.
Put a paper ball and soft cloth inside.
Move a toy near the holes so the cat can hunt with paws.
After 10 minutes, leave the box as a hideout.
Replace it when it starts falling apart.
dontDo: do not close the cat inside

title: Food hunt
summary: A scent game for an indoor cat.
steps: Hide a few pieces of dry food in the room.
Start with easy places.
Praise calmly when the cat finds food.
warnings: Count treats as part of the daily food amount.

title: Home scent puzzle for cats
summary: A calm food-search plan to reduce boredom.
steps: Prepare 5-8 tiny dry-food portions.
Hide them in safe easy places.
Let the cat search without rushing.
Make only one hiding place harder next time.
After play, refill water and let the cat rest.
dontDo: do not hide food in unsafe places

title: 5-minute play
summary: A short activity when the owner has little time.
steps: Pick one simple toy.
Do 3 minutes of movement and 2 minutes of calm-down.
End before the cat gets frustrated.
warnings: Several short sessions are better than one too-long session.

title: Micro-session: 5 minutes a day
summary: A minimum plan that is easy to repeat daily.
steps: Minute 1: slow tracking.
Minutes 2-3: short floor chase.
Minute 4: let the cat catch the toy.
Minute 5: treat, water and quiet.
Repeat in the evening if the cat still seeks activity.
dontDo: do not stop at peak excitement
do not use hands as toys

title: Quiet play for a calm cat
steps: Sit on the floor near the cat.
Move a soft toy slowly within paw reach.
Stop when the cat looks away or leaves.
warnings: Respect refusal — lack of play is information too.

title: Calm cat — no-pressure ritual
summary: A plan for cats that prefer watching over chasing.
steps: Prepare a quiet place without other animals.
Place a soft toy near the cat and move it minimally.
Add sniffing: hide one treat under paper.
Let the cat choose the pace.
Finish with water, litter box access and a quiet spot.
dontDo: do not lift the cat into play
do not punish a lack of reaction

title: Universal indoor play
summary: A safe start for most indoor cats.
steps: Choose a toy the cat already knows.
Do a short floor-level session.
Finish with a catch and rest.
warnings: Sudden behavior change should be discussed with a vet.

title: Indoor cat play plan
summary: A simple daily plan for home use.
steps: Morning: 5 minutes of light toy hunting.
Afternoon: one food or treat puzzle.
Evening: 10 minutes with a wand toy or box.
Once a week, rotate the toy to keep interest.
Observe which activities the cat chooses.
dontDo: do not ignore pain or stress signs
